facts: Three CAMELS members share static-attribute schema and Daymet-equivalent forcings; US 671 / DK 3,330 / IND ~470 catchments at daily resolution.
hypotheses: Transitivity holds: US→DK is approximately US→IND composed with IND→DK.
IND's monsoon-dominated regime introduces a mechanism shift orthogonal to the US↔DK covariate shift.
The asymmetry profile remains (3,3,2,0,2) for IND rather than monsoon dynamics forcing a profile extension.
intuitions: Cross-CAMELS analyses spanning all admitted members (US/DK/IND/CL/AUS/BR/CH/GB) likely exist.
unknowns: Whether 'country' is the right environment axis or whether it confounds 'climate regime', 'measurement-network design', and 'culture-of-hydrological-practice' cannot be determined.
